work_wit.alt_title: Menaion for September, October, and November (Byzantine, with local variations of the Antiochene region) (incomplete)

excerpt.as_written: ܒܝܕ ܘܣܘܝܥܐ ܕܬܠܝܬܝܘܬܐ ܩܕܝܫܬܐ ܣܓܝܕܐ ܘܫܘܝܬ̤ ܒܐܘܣܝܐ ܘܒܐܝܬܘܬܐ܇ ܟܬܒܝܢܢ ܛܪܘܦܘܠܘܓܝܢ ܕܩܢܘܢܐ ܕܟܪܘܟܝܗ̇ ܕܫܢܬܐ ܟܘܠܗ̇ ܐܝܟ ܪܘܫܡܐ ܕܝ̈ܘܢܝܐ. ܡܫܪܐ ܕܝܢ ܡܢ ܐܝܠܘܠ ܝܪܚܐ. ܕܗܘ ܐܝܬܘܗܝ ܪܝܫ ܫܢܬܐ
translation: With help and assistance of the Holy Trinity, venerable and united in essence and substance, we write Tropologion of the canons of the whole year according to the indication of the Greeks. We start then from the month of Ēlūl (September) which is the beginning of the year

contents.label: [Ēlūl (September)]
note: [1. St Symeon the Stylite]: [Kathismata], various modes (ff. 1v–2r, acephalous, continues presumably from †Leuven KU Library G-151 (ex-Hiersemann 500, No. 34)); Stichera, various modes (f. 2r–v); Canon, mode 8 (ff. 2r–5r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Another canon on St Symeon the Stylite, mode 4 (ff. 5r–8r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Canon on the Beginning of the year, mode 1 (ff. 9r–11r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9

2. Holy Martyr Mamas of Caesarea (ff. 11r–15r): Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 4 (f. 11r); Kathisma, mode 8 (f. 11r–v); Stichera, mode 8 (ff. 11v–12r); Canon, mode 4 (ff. 12r–15r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 15r)

4. Holy Martyr Babylas, the Archbishop of Antioch (ff. 16r–19v): Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 4 (f. 16r); Kathisma, mode 8 (f. 16r); Stichera, various modes (ff. 16r–17r); Canon, mode 6 (ff. 17r–19v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 19v)

5. Holy Martyrs Thouthail and Boethius (ff. 19v–27r): Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 5 (incipit only) (f. 19v); Kathisma on St Thouthail, mode 1 (ff. 19v–20r); Another [kathisma] on St Boetius, mode 8 (f. 20r); Stichera on St Thouthail, mode 8 (f. 20r–v); Other [stichera] on St Boetius, mode 4 (f. 20v–21r); Canon on St Thouthail, mode 8 (ff. 21r–23v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 23v); Canon on St Boethius mode 8 (ff. 23v–27r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 27r)
6. Archangel Michael (f. 27r–v): See akolouthia of 8 Teshrīn II (November)

7. Venerable Abraham, a former robber who repented in a women’s monastery (f. 27v–31r): Kathisma on God is the Lord, mode 2 (incipit only) (f. 27v); Kathisma, mode 8 (f. 27v); Stichera, various modes (ff. 27v–28v); Canon, mode 4 (ff. 28v–31r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 31r)

8. Nativity of the Mother of God (ff. 31v–37v): Kathisma on God is the Lord, mode 1 (f. 31v); Kathismata, various modes (ff. 31v–32r); Stichera, various modes (ff. 32r–33r); Canon, mode 2, composition by the Venerable Father (…) (ff. 33v–35v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Another canon on the Mother of God, composition by the Venerable (…), mode 4 (ff. 35v–37r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilaria (f. 37r–v)

9. Holy and Righteous Parents of the Mother of God Joachim and Anna (ff. 37v–40r): Kathisma on God is the Lord and other kathismata are the same as above (f. 37v); Stichera, mode 2 (ff. 37v–38r); Canon, mode 2 (ff. 38r–40r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion as above (f. 40r)

10. [Barhadbeshabba] (written in a later hand then erased; see Vat. sir. 647, f. 45r: “Venerable Barhadbeshabba who was blind and could see”) (ff. 40r–43v): Stichera, mode 5 (f. 40r); Canon, mode 5 (ff. 40r–43v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 43v)

13. Dedication of the Church of the Lord’s Resurrection in Jerusalem (ff. 43v–48r): Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 4 (f. 43v); Kathismata, various modes (ff. 43v–44r); Stichera, various modes (ff. 44r–45v); Canon, mode 4 (ff. 45v–48r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 48r)

14. Exaltation of the Holy Cross; passing away of the Archbishop of Constantinople St John Chrysostom, a native of God’s city of Antioch in Syria – see the akolouthia of bishops on 13 Teshrīn II (November) or 27 Kānūn II (January) (ff. 48r–54r): Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 4 (f. 48v); Kathismata, various modes (ff. 48v–49r); Stichera, various modes (ff. 49r–50r); Canon to the Feast of the Cross, mode 8, composition of father John Damascene bar Manṣūr (ff. 50v–53v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9: Another heirmos of the same ode [9], mode 8 (f. 53v); Exapostilaria (f. 54r)

16. Holy Martyr Euphemia (ff. 54r–57v): Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 4 (f. 54r); Kathisma, mode 1 (f. 54r–v); Stichera, various modes (ff. 54v–55v); Canon, mode 8 (ff. 55v–57v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 57v)

17. Holy Martyr Sophia (Wisdom) and her three daughters: Pistis (Faith), Elpis (Hope) and Agape (Love) (ff. 57v–61v): Troparion on God is the Lord (title only) (f. 57v); Kathisma, mode 1 (f. 58r); Stichera, mode 4 (ff. 58r–v); Canon, mode 1 (ff. 58v–61v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 61v)

20. Holy Martyr Eustathius, his wife and sons, [martyred] in Rome (ff. 62r–65v): Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 4 (incipit only) (f. 62r); Kathisma, mode 8 (f. 62r); Stichera, mode 2 (f. 62r–v); Canon, mode 8 (ff. 62v–65r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilaria (f. 65r–v)

21. Feast of the Mother of God when She has performed a miracle on the dragon that she killed, in the monastery known as Daphnūnā on the Black Mountain in the region of Antioch (ff. 65v–68v): Stichera, mode 1 (f. 65v–66r); Canon on the Mother of God and the Holy Martyr Thecla, mode 4 (ff. 66r–68v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 68v)

22. Holy Martyr Phokas and his disciple John (ff. 68v–72r): Kathisma, mode 5 (f. 68v); Stichera, mode 8 (ff. 68v–69r); Canon, mode 8 (ff. 69r–71v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (ff. 71v–72r)

23. Annunciation to the priest Zechariah about [the conception of] St John the Baptist (ff. 72r–75v): Kathisma on God is the Lord, mode 6 (f. 72r); Kathismata, various modes (f. 72r–v); Stichera, various modes (ff. 72v–73r); Canon, mode 6 (ff. 73r–75v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 75v)

24. Holy Protomartyr Thecla (ff. 75v–79r): “Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 4 (incipit only), see 16 [Ēlūl], Holy Martyr Euphemia” (f. 75v–76r); Kathisma, mode 8 (f. 76r); Stichera, mode 2 (f. 76r–v); Canon, mode 8 (ff. 76v–79r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilaria (f. 79r)

27. [Feast of] Our Lady Mary, the Helper of Martyrs and Saints (ff. 80r–84r): Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 4 (incipit only) (f. 80r); Kathisma, mode 1 (f. 80r); Stichera, mode 8 (f. 80r–v); Canon, mode 8 (ff. 80v–84r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (blank space filled in a later hand) (f. 84r)

28. St Chariton of Jerusalem (ff. 84r–88r): Kathisma on God is the Lord (blank space filled in a later hand) (f. 84r–v); Kathisma, mode 8 (f. 84v); Stichera, mode 4 (ff. 84v–85r); Canon, mode 4 (ff. 85v–88r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (blank space filled in a later hand) (f. 88r)

29. Passing away of the Venerable Cyriacus of Jerusalem (ff. 88r–91v); Kathisma on God is the Lord, mode 2 (incipit only) (f. 88r); Kathisma, common for the venerable (no text) (f. 88r); Stichera, mode 8 (f. 88r–v); Canon, mode 8 (ff. 88r–91v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion, [common] to the venerable (no text, addition in a later hand) (f. 91v)

contents.label: Teshrīn I (October)
note: 1. Apostle Ananias who baptized Apostle Paul (ff. 91v–94v): Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 6 (f. 91v); Kathisma, mode 1 (f. 91v); Stichera, mode 2 (ff. 91v–92r); Canon, mode 8 (ff. 92r–94v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion, [common] to the apostles (no text) (f. 94v)

2. Bishop Cyprian and Justina the Virgin (ff. 94v–100r): Kathisma on God is the Lord, mode (…) (incipit only) (f. 94v); Kathisma, mode 8 (ff. 94v–95r); Stichera, various modes (ff. 95r–97r); Canon, mode 4 (ff. 97r–100r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilaria (f. 100r)

6. Apostle Thomas from the Twelve, named the Twin (ff. 101r–104v): Kathisma on God is the Lord, mode 3 (f. 101r); Kathismata, mode 8 (f. 101r); Stichera, mode 8 (f. 101r–v); Canon on the apostle, mode 4 (ff. 102r–104v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 104v)

7. Great Martyrs Sergius and Bacchus (ff. 104v–112v): Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 5 (f. 104v); Kathismata, various modes (f. 105r–v); Stichera, various modes (ff. 105v–107v); Canon, mode 1 (ff. 107v–109v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Another canon on martyrs, mode 8 (ff. 110r–112v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 112v–116v)

<9. Holy Martyrs Stratonicus and Seleucus> (title not preserved; see Vat. sir. 647, f. 45r): “Troparion on God is the Lord to Holy Martyrs Stratonicus and (…), mode 4, see 16 Ēlūl, on Holy Martyr Euphemia; another, mode 5, see 7 [Teshrīn I], on Holy Martyrs Sergius and Bacchus” (incipits only) (f. 113r); Kathisma, mode 8 (f. 113r); Stichera, various modes (f. 113r–114r); Canon, mode 8 (ff. 114r–116v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 116v)

10. Venerable Euthalius (ff. 116v–119v): Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 2 (incipit only) (f. 116v); Kathisma, mode 5 (f. 116v–117r); Stichera, mode 5 (ff. 117r); Canon, mode 5 (ff. 117r–119v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 119v)

11. Seven [Ecumenical] Councils (ff. 119v–125r): Kathisma on God is the Lord (no text, addition in a later hand) (f. 119v); Kathisma, mode 8 (f. 119v); Stichera, mode 8 (ff. 120r); Canon, mode 8 (ff. 120v–124v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilaria (f. 124v–125r)

13. Holy Martyr Eustathius of Qenneshrin (ff. 125r–129r): Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 3 (incipit only) (f. 125r); Kathisma, mode 4 (f. 125r); Stichera, mode 2 (f. 125r–v); Canon, mode 4 (ff. 126r–128v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (ff. 128v–129r)

18. Apostle Luke the Evangelist (ff. 129r–133r): Kathisma on God is the Lord, mode 3 (incipit only) (f. 129r); Kathisma, mode 8 (f. 129r); Stichera, mode 8 (ff. 129r–130r); Canon, mode 8 (ff. 130r–133r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 133r)

19. Holy Martyr Bishop Conon and Holy Martyr Varus (ff. 133v–141r): Kathismata, various modes (f. 133v); Stichera on the Bishop [Conon], mode 5 (ff. 133v–134r); Stichera on the Holy Martyr Varus, mode 1 (f. 134v); Canon on the Bishop and Martyr Conon, mode 4 (ff. 134v–137v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Canon on the Holy Martyr Varus, mode 6 (ff. 137v–141r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilaria (f. 141r)

21. Passing away of Venerable Hilarion the Great (ff. 141v–145r): Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 2 (incipit only) (f. 141v); Kathisma, mode 8 (f. 141v); Stichera, mode 4 (f. 141v–142r); Canon, mode 5 (ff. 142r–145r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (ff. 145r)

23. Apostle James, the Brother of Our Lord (ff. 145r–148v): Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 3 (incipit only) (f. 145r); Kathisma, mode 8 (f. 145r); Stichera, mode 1 (f. 145r–v); Canon, mode 8 (ff. 145v–148v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (ff. 148v)

26. Holy Martyr Demetrius, the Finding of the Head of St John the Baptist, and the memory of the earthquake in the time of the Emperor Leo [III] (Constantinople, A.D. 740) (ff. 149r–159v): Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 3, on the Martyr Demetrius (f. 149r); Another [troparion on God is the Lord], on the Head of St John the Baptist, mode 4 (f. 149r); “Kathisma to the martyrs chant either on St Gregory or St Panteleimon; on St John [the Baptist] see stichera and kathismata on 24 Shbāṭ (February)” (f. 149r–v); Stichera on the Holy Martyr Demetrius, mode 8 (ff. 149v–150r); Another [sticheron] on the earthquake, mode 6, composition of Venerable Symeon the Stylite who performed miracles in the sea, in God’s city of Antioch; composed at the time of an earthquake (Antioch, A.D. 526) (f. 150r–v); Canon on St Demetrius, mode 4 (ff. 150v–153r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Another canon on St Demetrius, mode 6 (ff. 153r–156v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 156v); Canon on the Finding of the Head of St John the Baptist, mode 6 (ff. 156v–159v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 159v)

contents.label: Teshrīn II (November)
note: 1. Holy Unmercenaries Cosmas and Damian (ff. 159v–165v): Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 8 (f. 159v); Kathisma, mode 8 (f. 159v); Stichera, mode 6 (f. 159v–160v); Canon on Saints Cosmas and Damian, mode 4 (ff. 160v–163r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Another canon on Saints Cosmas and Damian, mode 6 (ff. 163r–163r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 165v)

3. Dedication of the Church of St George [in Lydda] (ff. 165v–169r): Kathismata and stichera see for 13 Ēlūl (September) (f. 165v); Other stichera, mode 4 (f. 165v); Canon, mode 2 (ff. 165v–169r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 169r)

8. Archangel Michael (ff. 169r–175r): Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 4 (f. 169r); Kathisma, mode 8 (f. 169r); Stichera, various modes (f. 169v–170r); Canon on the Archangel, mode 5 (ff. 170v–172v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Another canon on the Archangel, mode 8 (ff. 172v–175r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 175r)

11. Holy Martyr Menas of Alexandria (ff. 175r–178r): “Troparion on God is the Lord, say common” (f. 175r); Kathisma, mode 5 (f. 175r–v); Stichera, mode 1 (f. 175v); Canon, mode 4 (ff. 175v–178r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; “Exapostilarion common for martyrs or, if you wish, chant to St George or another martyr” (f. 178r)

12. Archbishop St John the Merciful (ff. 178r–181r): Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 5 (f. 178r); Kathisma, mode 8 (f. 178r); Stichera, mode 4 (f. 178v); Canon, mode 4 (ff. 178v–181r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9

13. Archbishop St John Chrysostom (ff. 181r–188r): Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 8 (f. 181r); Kathisma, mode 8 (f. 181r); Stichera, mode 4, 2 sets (ff. 181r–182r); “Other stichera are written for 27 Kānūn II (January)” (no text) (f. 182r); Canon, mode 2 (ff. 182r–184v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Another canon on St John Chrysostom, composition of Father John Damascene bar Manṣūr, mode 8 (ff. 184v–188r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; “Exapostilarion – the same as for bishops” (no text) (f. 188r)

14. Apostle Philip from the Twelve (ff. 188v–191r): Kathisma on God is the Lord, mode 3 (incipit only) (f. 188v); “The rest of kathismata – chant the common on apostles” (no text) (f. 188v); Stichera, mode 8 (ff. 188v); Canon, mode 6, composition of the Venerable Theophanes (ff. 188v–191r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9

15. Holy Confessors Shamuna, Guria and Habib from Edessa (ff. 191v–196v): Kathisma on God is the Lord, mode 5 (incipit only) (f. 191v); Another kathisma, mode 8 (f. 191v); Stichera, mode 6 (ff. 191v–192r); Canon, mode 8 (ff. 192r–196v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 196v)
Bishop St Gregory Thaumaturgus (ff. 197r–199r): Canon, mode 2 (ff. 197r–199r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 199r)

18. Holy Martyrs Plotinus and Romanus (ff. 199v–205v): Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 5, “[see the text] written on 7 Teshrīn I” (f. 199v); Kathisma on Holy Martyr Plotinus, mode 8 (f. 199v); Another [kathisma] on Holy Martyr Romanus, mode 1 (f. 199v); “Stichera chant either common for martyrs or whatever you wish” (no text) (ff. 199v); Canon on Holy Martyr Plotinus, mode 8 (ff. 199v–202v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Another canon on Holy Martyr Romanus and another martyr [commemorated] on the same day, mode 1 (ff. 202v–205v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 205v)

21. Entry of the Mother of God into the Temple (ff. 206r–211r): Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 4 (f. 206r); Kathismata, various modes (f. 206r–v); Stichera, various modes (ff. 206v–207v); Canon, mode 4 (ff. 207v–211r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 211r)

25. St Peter, the Archbishop of Alexandria (ff. 212r–215r): Troparion on God is the Lord (incipit only) (f. 212r); Kathismata, mode 8 (f. 212r); Stichera, mode 4 (f. 212r–v); Canon, mode 4 (ff. 212v–214v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion (f. 215r)

27. Venerable Palladius, native of the Blessed City of Edessa, and Holy Martyr Jacob, who was mutilated in Persia (ff. 215r–222r): Troparion to the venerable, mode 2 (incipit only); another [troparion], mode 1 (incipit only); troparion to the martyrs, mode 3 (f. 215r); Kathismata to the venerable, various modes (f. 215r–v); Kathisma to the martyr, mode 1 (f. 215v); Stichera to the venerable, various modes (ff. 215v–216v); Stichera to the martyr, mode 6 (ff. 216v–217r); Canon to the Venerable Palladius, mode 8 (ff. 217r–219v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Canon to [the Holy Martyr Jacob] the Mutilated, mode 8 (ff. 219v–222r), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Exapostilarion to the Venerable Palladius (f. 222r); [Exapostilarion] to the martyr – chant either on the martyrs or whatever you wish (no text) (f. 222r)

30. Apostle Andrew, from the Twelve, brother of the Supreme Apostle Peter (ff. 222r–227v) (incomplete): Kathisma on God is the Lord, mode 3 (incipit only) (f. 222r); Kathisma, mode 8 (f. 222r); Stichera, various modes (f. 222r–223r); Canon, composition by Father John Damascene bar Manṣūr, mode 1 (ff. 223v–227v), Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Another canon to the Apostle Andrew, mode 2 (f. 227v), Ode 1 (incomplete)
